title: To Liberty
content:
Here's to our Goddess, Liberty,
    Idol of bronze and stone!
    May she awake to life some day
    And let her charms be known.

title: The Milk Jug
content:
The Gentle Milk Jug blue and white
        I love with all my soul,
    She pours herself with all her might
        To fill my breakfast bowl.

title: The Hippopotamus.
content:
"OH, say, what is this fearful, wild
    In-cor-ri-gible cuss?"
    "This crea-ture (don't say 'cuss,' my child;
    'T is slang) - this crea-t

title: A Kitten's Fancy
content:
The Kitten mews outside the Door,
         The Cat-bird in the Tree,
    The Sea-mew mews upon the Shore,
         The Catfish in the Sea.

title: Happy Thought
content:
The world is so full of a number of Mice
    I'm sure that we all should be happy and nice.

title: The Dove Of Peace
content:
Here's to the Dove of Peace!
    May she find a mate some day,
    And may her tribe increase
    As fast as she can lay!


    With cooing do

title: The Sea Serpent
content:
O wondrous worm that won the Height
    Of Fame by keeping out of sight!
    Never was known on Land or Sea
    Such a Colossal Modesty;
    N

title: Oh, Editor, Editor,
content:
Oh, Editor, Editor,
    Awful and grand,
    Who holdest our fate
    In the palm of thy hand,
    Dost ever reflect
    How one day thy ghos

title: An Alphabet of Celebrities
content:
A's Albert Edward, well meaning but flighty,
    Who invited King Arthur, the blameless and mighty,
    To meet Alcibiades and Aphrodite.

title: The Cat.
content:
OB-SERVE the Cat up-on this page.
    Phil-os-o-phers in ev-er-y age,
    The ver-y wis-est of the wise,
    Have tried her mind to an-a-lyze

title: An Inquiry
content:
A Birdie cocked his little head,
        Winked his eye at me and said,
    "Say, are you a Pussy Willer,
        Or just a Kitty-Catty pillar?

title: The Dolphin
content:
The Dolphin was, if you should wish
    To call him so,--the King of Fish.
    Though having neither gills nor scales,
    His title should be

title: Some Geese.
content:
EV-ER-Y child who has the use
    Of his sen-ses knows a goose.
    See them un-der-neath the tree
    Gath-er round the goose-girl's knee,

title: To The Typewriter
content:
Here's to the Typewriter!
    Health to her type!
    Whether blond or brunette
    Or budding or ripe.
    If she be the right type
    Be s

title: Brander Matthews
content:
I'd best beware how I make free
    With Brander Matthews L. L. D.
    Since Prexy Wilson's paved the way
    He may be President some day.

title: The Fly.
content:
OB-SERVE, my child, the House-hold Fly,
    With his ex-traor-di-na-ry eye:
    What-ev-er thing he may be-hold
    Is mul-ti-plied a thou-sand

title: J. Forbes-Robertson
content:
I'm told the Artist who aspires
    To draw Forbes-Robertson requires
    A Sargent's brush. Dear me! how sad!
    I've lost the only one I had

title: To Her Shadow
content:
Here's to her shadow!
    May it mark the hours
    Upon the sundial of her life--in flowers!

title: In Darkest Africa
content:
At evening when the lamp is lit,
        The tired Human People sit
    And doze, or turn with solemn looks
        The speckled pages of their

title: The Unicorn
content:
The Unicorn 's a first-rate sort.
    He helps the Lion to support
    The royal arms of England's King
    And keep the Throne from tottering.

title: Stairs - A Toast
content:
Here's to the man who invented stairs
    And taught our feet to soar!
    He was the first who ever burst
    Into a second floor.

    The w

title: The Ant.
content:
MY child, ob-serve the use-ful Ant,
    How hard she works each day.
    She works as hard as ad-a-mant
    (That's very hard, they say).

title: The Rub�iy�t of a Persian Kitten
content:
Wake! for the Golden Cat has put to flight
    The Mouse of Darkness with his Paw of Light:
    Which means, in Plain and simple every-day

title: The Mongoos.
content:
THIS, Chil-dren, is the famed Mon-goos.
    He has an ap-pe-tite ab-struse;
    Strange to re-late, this crea-ture takes
    A cu-ri-ous joy in

title: Education
content:
When People think that Kittens play,
         It's really quite the other way.
    For when they chase the Ball or Bobbin
         They learn t

title: To Our Lady Nicotine
content:
Here's to Lady Nicotine!
    Saint and Sorceress and Queen!
    Saint, whose purple halo rings
    Lift our eyes from earthly things;
    Witc

title: Guglielmo Marconi
content:
I like Marconi best to see
    Beneath a Macaroni tree
    Playing that Nocturne in F Sharp
    By Chopin, on a Wireless Harp.

title: A Whale.
content:
THE con-sci-en-tious art-ist tries
    On-ly to draw what meets his eyes.
    This is the Whale; he seems to be
    A spout of wa-ter in the se

title: Kitten's Night Thought
content:
When Human Folk put out the light,
         And think they've made it dark as night,
    A Pussy Cat sees every bit
         As well as when th

title: Mark Twain: A Pipe Dream
content:
Well I recall how first I met
        Mark Twain - an infant barely three
    Rolling a tiny cigarette
        While cooing on his nurse's knee

title: The Hyppogriff
content:
Biologists are prone to sniff
    At hybrids like the Hyppogriff.
    In evolution's plan, they say,
    There is no place for such as they.

title: The Sloth.
content:
The Sloth en-joys a life of Ease;
    He hangs in-vert-ed from the trees,
    And views life up-side down.
    If you, my child, are noth-ing l

title: Winter and Summer
content:
In Winter when the air is chill,
        And winds are blowing loud and shrill,
    All snug and warm I sit and purr,
        Wrapped in my ove

title: John Drew
content:
For Perfect Form there are but few
    That can compare with Mr. Drew;
    A Form most fittingly displayed
    In r�les from London, tailor-mad

title: Christopher Columbus
content:
Columbus is an easy one
    To draw, for when the picture's done,
    Where is the captious critic who
    Can say the likeness is not true?

title: G. K. Chesterton
content:
When Plain Folk, such as you or I,
    See the Sun sinking in the sky,
    We think it is the Setting Sun,
    But Mr. Gilbert Chesterton

title: Phyllis Lee
content:
Beside a Primrose 'broider'd Rill
        Sat Phyllis Lee in Silken Dress
    Whilst Lucius limn'd with loving skill
        Her likeness, as a

title: The Rhinoceros.
content:
SO this is the Rhi-no-ce-ros!
    I won-der why he looks so cross.
    Per-haps he is an-noyed a bit
    Be-cause his cloth-ing does not fit.

title: Ignace Jan Paderewski
content:
When Paderewski is forgot,
    Our children's children, like as not,
    Will worship in the Hall of Fame,
    Some great piano-maker's name.

title: The Game
content:
Watching a ball on the end of a string,
        Watching it swing back and to,
    Oh, I do think it the pleasantest thing
        Ever a Kitte

title: To Temptation
content:
Here's to temptation!
    Give us strength and grace
    Against her witching smile,
    To set our face!

title: To Hope
content:
Here's to Hope,
    the child of Care,
    And pretty sister
    of Despair!
    Here's hoping that
    Hope's children shan't
    Take afte

title: Shakespeare
content:
Will Shakespeare, the Baconians say,
    Was the Belasco of his day--
    Others more plausibly maintain
    He was the double of Hall Caine.

title: William Dean Howells
content:
Not squirrels in the park alone
    His love and winter-kindness own.
    When Literary Fledglings try
    Their wings, in first attempt to fly

title: Good and Bad Kittens
content:
Kittens, you are very little,
        And your kitten bones are brittle,
    If you'd grow to Cats respected,
        See your play be not negl

title: To Stern Critics
content:
Here's to stern Critics!
    May they some day learn
    The forward lookout's
    Better than the stern!

title: The Puppy
content:
The Puppy cannot mew or talk,
        He has a funny kind of walk,
    His tail is difficult to wag
        And that's what makes him walk zigz

title: Cerberus
content:
Dear Reader, should you chance to go
    To Hades, do not fail to throw
    A "Sop to Cerberus" at the gate,
    His anger to propitiate.

title: To The Publisher
content:
To The Publisher! - Drink!
    Let his virtue be shown
    In the Good Works of others
    If not in his own.

title: Charles W. Eliot
content:
And now comes Dr. Eliot stating
    That Hell won't bear investigating.
    It looks like Charlie's out to bust
    The Great Hell-Fire Insuran

title: William Howard Taft
content:
I'm sorry William Taft is out
    Of Politics; without a doubt
    Of all the Presidential crew
    He was the easiest to do.

title: The Leopard.
content:
THIS is the Le-o-pard, my child;
    His tem-per's any-thing but mild.
    The Le-o-pard can't change his spots,
    And that - so say the Hot-

title: F. W. Hohenzollern
content:
In things like this I've always tried
    To look upon the Brighter Side;
    And when I see the Prince, I say
    "The Crown's worth something

title: Israel Zangwill
content:
This picture though it is not much
        Like Zangwill, is not void of worth
    It has one true Zangwillian touch
        It looks like noth

title: To The Maid With Fancy Free
content:
Here's to the maid with Fancy Free;
    If Cupid's necromancy
    Imprison not her heart, maybe,
    It will arrest her Fancy.

title: Arnold Bennett
content:
'Tis very comforting to know
    That every other day or so
    A Book by Bennett will appear
    To charm the Western Hemisphere.
    I see h

title: Anticipation
content:
When I grow up I mean to be
    A Lion large and fierce to see.
    I'll mew so loud that Cook in fright
    Will give me all the cream in sigh

title: The Yak.
content:
THIS is the Yak, so neg-li-g�e:
    His coif-fure's like a stack of hay;
    He lives so far from Any-where,
    I fear the Yak neg-lects his h

title: The Centaur
content:
The Centaur led a double life:
    Two natures in perpetual strife
    He had, that never could agree
    On what the bill-of-fare should be;

title: A Seal.
content:
SEE, chil-dren, the Fur-bear-ing Seal;
    Ob-serve his mis-di-rect-ed zeal:
    He dines with most ab-ste-mi-ous care
    On Fish, Ice Water a

title: J. Pierpont Morgan
content:
In Rome, when Morgan came to town,
        They nailed the Colosseum down.
        A great Collector! Once his Fad
        Was Coins, but w

title: The Outing
content:
My Bed is like a little Bark,
         The hatch is battened down,
    And in the basket cabin dark
         I sail away from Town.

    Now,

title: Theodore Roosevelt
content:
The ways of Providence are odd.
    If THEODORE means "The Gift of God,"
    Let us give thanks, at any rate,
    The Gift was not a duplicate.

title: The Joy Ride
content:
When Mistress Peggy moves around,
        Her dresses make a mocking sound.
    "You can't catch me!" they seem to say--
        I often steal

title: Pegasus
content:
The ancients made no end of fuss
    About a horse named Pegasus,
    A famous flyer of his time,
    Who often soared to heights sublime,

title: Facilis Ascensus
content:
Up into the Cherry Tree,
        Who should climb but little me,
    With both my Paws I hold on tight,
        And look upon a pleasant sight.

title: A Penguin.
content:
THE Pen-guin sits up-on the shore
    And loves the lit-tle fish to bore;
    He has one en-er-vat-ing joke
    That would a very Saint pro-vok

title: The Wolf.
content:
OH, yes, the Wolf is bad, it's true;
    But how with-out him could we do?
    If there were not a wolf, what good
    Would be the tale of RID

title: To Our Sweethearts
content:
To our Sweethearts and Wives,
    The joy of our lives!
    May our Wives be our Sweethearts--
    Our Sweethearts, our Wives.

title: The Ph[oe]nix
content:
The Ph[oe]nix was, as you might say,
    The burning question of his day:
    The more he burned, the more he grew
    Splendiferous in feather

title: An Ostrich.
content:
THIS is an Os-trich. See him stand:
    His head is bur-ied in the sand.
    It is not that he seeks for food,
    Nor is he shy, nor is he rud

title: David Belasco
content:
Behold Belasco in his den,
    Wielding the scissors, paste and pen,
    And writing with consummate skill
    A play by W. De Mille.

title: To The Clock
content:
Here's to the Clock!
    Whose hands, we pray heaven,
    When we come home at three,
    Have stopped at eleven!

title: Medusa
content:
How did Medusa do her hair?
    The question fills me with despair.
    It must have caused her sore distress
    That head of curling snakes t

title: The Elephant.
content:
This is the El-e-phant, who lives
    With but one aim - to please.
    His i-vo-ry tusk he free-ly gives
    To make pi-a-no keys.
    One gr

title: Henrik Ibsen
content:
I once drew Ibsen, looking bored
    Across a deep Norwegian Fjord,
    And very nearly every one
    Mistook him for the midnight sun.

title: The Cockatrice
content:
If you will listen to advice
    You will avoid the Cockatrice--
    A caution I need hardly say
    Wholly superfluous to-day.
    Yet had yo

title: To Our Readers
content:
Here's to our Readers, Health! good Looks!
    And Joy ad infinitum
    And may they live to read our Books
    As long as we may write 'em.

title: The Whole Duty of Kittens
content:
When Human Folk at Table eat,
         A Kitten must not mew for meat,
    Or jump to grab it from the Dish,
         (Unless it happens to be

title: The Hydra
content:
The Hydra Hercules defied,
    Its nine diminished heads must hide
    Before the baneful modern beast
    Who has a thousand heads at least.

title: The Lion
content:
The Lion does not move at all,
        Winter or Summer, Spring or Fall,
    He does not even stretch or yawn,
        But lies in silence on t

title: The Gryphon
content:
It chanced that Allah, looking round,
    When he had made his creatures, found
    Half of an Eagle and a pair
    Of extra Lion legs to spare

title: The Mermaid
content:
Although a Fishwife in a sense,
    She does not barter Fish for Pence.
    Fisher of Men, her Golden Nets
    For foolish Sailormen she sets.

title: The Platypus.
content:
MY child, the Duck-billed Plat-y-pus
    A sad ex-am-ple sets for us:
    From him we learn how In-de-ci-sion
    Of char-ac-ter pro-vokes De-r

title: Hafiz
content:
When Hafiz saw the portrait free,
    By Monty Flagg, of him and me,
    He made remarks one can't repeat
    In any reputable sheet.

title: The Chimera
content:
You'd think a lion or a snake
    Were quite enough one's nerves to shake;
    But in this classic beast we find
    A lion and a snake combine

title: The Floor
content:
Here's to the floor,
    Our best friend of all,
    Who sticks to us close
    In the time of our fall.
    When benches are fickle
    And

title: The Pig-Pen.
content:
OH, turn not from the hum-ble Pig,
    My child, or think him in-fra dig.
    We oft hear lit-er-a-ry men
    Boast of the in-flu-ence of the P

title: The Siren
content:
The Siren may be said to be
    The Chorus-Lady of the Sea;
    Tho' Mermaids claim her as their kin,
    Instead of fishy tail and fin
    Tw

title: A Mole.
content:
SEE, chil-dren, the mis-guid-ed Mole.
    He lives down in a deep, dark hole;
    Sweet-ness, and Light, and good Fresh Air
    Are things for

title: Dante
content:
If you should ask me, whether Dante
    Drank Benedictine or Chianti,
    I should reply, "I cannot say,
    But I can draw him either way."

title: The Puncture
content:
When I was just a Kitten small,
        They gave to me a Rubber Ball
    To roll upon the floor.
        One day I tapped it with my paw

title: George Ade
content:
Somehow I always like to think
    Of GEORGEADE as a Summer Drink,
    Sparkling and cool, with just a Tang
    Of Pleasant Effervescent Slang;

title: To Music
content:
Here's to Music,
    Joy of joys!
    One man's music's
    Another man's noise.

title: George Bernard Shaw
content:
The very name of Bernard Shaw
    Fills me with mingled Mirth and Awe.
    Mixture of Mephistopheles,
    Don Quixote, and Diogenes,
    The D

title: A Thought
content:
It's very nice to think of how
    In every country lives a Cow
    To furnish milk with all her might
    For Kittens' comfort and delight.

title: Daniel Frohman
content:
I love to picture Daniel Frohman
    In costume of a noble Roman.
    For Dan has just the style of hair,
    That Julius C�sar used to wear.

title: The Harpy
content:
They certainly contrived to raise
    Queer ladies in the olden days.
    Either the type had not been fixed,
    Or else Zoology got mixed.

title: Cloud,The, An Idyll Of The Western Front
content:
I

    |Scene|: A wayside shrine in France.

    |Persons|: Celeste, Pierre, a Cloud.

    |Celeste| (gazing at

title: The Dog
content:
The Dog is black or white or brown
        And sometimes spotted like a clown.
    He loves to make a foolish noise
        And Human Company e

title: To Fashion
content:
Fashion! Lovely Dame!
    Pledge in sparkling wine!
    Let us add her name
    To the Muses' nine!

    Though the lovely Nin
    All should

title: The Sphinx
content:
She was half Lady and half cat--
    What is so wonderful in that?
    Half of our lady friends (so say
    The other half) are Cats to-day.

title: Foreign Kittens
content:
Kittens large and Kittens small,
    Prowling on the Back Yard Wall,
    Though your fur be rough and few,
    I should like to play with you.

title: The Peter Pan Alphabet
content:
The Lord forgive if we transgress
    Thus to familiarly address
        One of our betters.
    But Jamie, do you no recall
    The slate w

title: The Jinn
content:
To call a Jinn the only thing
    One needed was a magic ring.
    You rubbed the ring and forth there came
    A monster born of smoke and fla

title: Here's Looking
content:
Here's looking
    at you, dear!
    though I should pour
    A sea of wine,
    my eyes would
    thirst for more.

title: John S. Sargent
content:
Here's Sargent doing the Duchess X
    In pink velours and pea-green checks.
    "It helps," says he, "to lift your Grace
    A bit above the c

title: Peter Dunne
content:
By the Harp

    "Shpeaking of Harps, sure me frind Pete
     Has got the Harp of Tara beat,"
     Said Mr. Dooley. "Div'l a thing
     That boy c

title: The Minotaur
content:
No book of monsters is complete
    Without the Minotaur of Crete.
    Yet should I draw him you would quail,
    So in his place I draw a veil

title: The Shadow Kitten
Napoleon
content:
I like to draw Napoleon best
    Because one hand is in his vest,
    The other hand behind his back.
    (For drawing hands I have no knack.)

title: To The Creditor
content:
Here's to the Creditor,
    Long may he reign!
    May his Faith never waver,
    His Trust never wane.
    May the Lord make him gentle

title: Rain
content:
The rain is raining everywhere,
        Kittens to shelter fly -
    But Human Folk wear overshoes,
        To keep their hind paws dry.

title: The Giraffe.
content:
SEE the Gi-raffe; he is so tall
    There is not room to get him all
    U-pon the page. His head is high-er - 
    The pic-ture proves it - th

title: Rudyard Kipling
content:
I seem to see a Shining One,
    With eyes that gleam, now fierce, now tender,
    Through Goggles that reflect the Sun
    "With more than Ori

title: The Gargoyle
content:
The Gargoyle often makes its perch
    On a cathedral or a church,
    Where, mid ecclesiastic style,
    It smiles an early-Gothic smile.

title: The Smoker's Year Book
content:
JANUARY


Now Time the harvester surveys
His sorry crops of yesterdays;
Of trampled hopes and reaped regrets,
And for another harvest whets
His a

title: To The Waiter
content:
We drink your health, O Waiter!
    And may you be preserved
    From old age, gout, or sudden death!--
    At least till supper's served.

title: A Chameleon.
content:
A USE-FUL les-son you may con,
    My Child, from the Cha-me-le-on:
    He has the gift, ex-treme-ly rare
    In an-i-mals, of sav-oir-faire.

title: The Moon
content:
The Moon is like a big round cheese
        That shines above the garden trees,
    And like a cheese grows less each night,
        As though

title: Saint Paul
content:
It saddens me to think Saint Paul
    Such lengthy letters had to scrawl.
    And so to make his labor lighter
    I picture him with a typewri

title: Gilbert K. Chesterton
content:
Unless I'm very much misled,
    Chesterton's easier done than said.
    I have not seen him, but his looks
    I can imagine from his books.

title: To Neptune
content:
A health to King Neptune,
    The boss of the wave!
    Who sits on the Ocean
    And makes it behave.
    Come fill up your bumpers
    And

title: Hiram Maxim
content:
From Hiram Maxim's hair you'd think
    His specialty was spilling ink--
    You'd never dream he'd spilt more blood
    Than any one man since

title: An Arctic Hare.
content:
AN Arc-tic Hare we now be-hold.
    The hair, you will ob-serve, is white;
    But if you think the Hare is old,
    You will be ver-y far from

title: The Golden Cat
content:
Great is the Golden Cat who treads
         The Blue Roof Garden o'er our heads,
    The never tired smiling One
         That Human People cal

title: The Salamander
content:
The Salamander made his bed
    Among the glowing embers red.
    A Fiery Furnace, to his mind,
    Hygiene and Luxury combined.
    He was, i

title: John D. Rockefeller
content:
Few faces interest me less
    Than Rockefeller's, I confess.
    'Twould vastly better suit my whim
    To draw his bank account, than him.

title: The Satyr
content:
The Satyr lived in times remote,
    A shape half-human and half-goat,
    Who, having all Man's faults combined
    With a Goat's nature unref

title: The Chimpanzee.
content:
CHIL-DREN, be-hold the Chim-pan-zee:
    He sits on the an-ces-tral tree
    From which we sprang in ag-es gone.
    I'm glad we sprang: had we
